title: Quiz: QAOA Variants and Extensions
description: Test your understanding of QAOA variants including Warm-Start, Recursive, and Multi-Angle QAOA

questions.question: What is the key idea behind Warm-Start QAOA?

options.text: Starting from a state biased toward a classical solution

questions.explanation: Warm-Start QAOA initializes from a state biased toward a classical approximate solution rather than the uniform superposition, providing a better starting point.

questions.question: How does Recursive QAOA (RQAOA) reduce problem complexity?

options.text: By fixing variables based on correlation measurements
By reducing the number of edges

questions.explanation: RQAOA iteratively fixes variables based on measured ZZ correlations from QAOA outputs, reducing the problem size until it can be solved classically.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of Multi-Angle QAOA over standard QAOA?

options.text: More expressibility at the same circuit depth

questions.explanation: Multi-Angle QAOA uses different angles for each term in the Hamiltonian, increasing expressibility without adding more layers.

questions.question: In RQAOA, if the ZZ correlation ⟨Z_iZ_j⟩ > 0, it suggests the variables should have the same value.
questions.explanation: A positive ZZ correlation indicates the qubits tend to be in the same state (both 0 or both 1), suggesting they should be assigned the same value.

questions.question: How many parameters per layer does standard QAOA have for a problem with n qubits and m edges?
questions.explanation: Standard QAOA uses just 2 parameters per layer: one γ for the cost Hamiltonian and one β for the mixer, regardless of problem size.

questions.question: When is Warm-Start QAOA most beneficial?

options.text: When a good classical approximation can be computed efficiently
When the problem is already solved

questions.explanation: Warm-Start QAOA is most useful when classical heuristics can provide a reasonable approximation, which can then be improved by the quantum algorithm.

questions.question: What is the Quantum Alternating Operator Ansatz?

options.text: A quantum algorithm for linear algebra
A generalization of QAOA with multiple phase and mixing operators

questions.explanation: The Quantum Alternating Operator Ansatz generalizes QAOA to allow multiple phase-separating and mixing operators, enabling multi-objective optimization.

questions.question: Which statements about QAOA variants are correct? (Select all)

options.text: Multi-Angle QAOA has fewer parameters than standard QAOA
Warm-Start QAOA modifies the initial state
All variants improve on standard QAOA in every case

questions.explanation: RQAOA runs QAOA multiple times to fix variables (a), and Warm-Start changes initialization (c). Multi-Angle has MORE parameters (not fewer), and no variant is universally better.
